title: crepis runcinata subsp. hallii
intro: Crepis runcinata subsp. hallii, also known as Hall's Hawksbeard, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its distinctive, toothed leaves and white to pinkish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Crepis runcinata subsp. hallii are said to include relief from digestive issues, reduction of inflammation, and support for the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in salves or ointments for topical applications.
